content:
Hi there! :)

I noticed that xa_alloc() was not offered in the final version of the XArray abstraction. Is there a particular reason behind this?

Also, I do recall it being present in a previous version, but I do not see it mentioned on the changelog of v19.

cc @Tamir Duberstein

Yes indeed, it wasn't included in the final version - I trimmed it to cut scope. I have a local branch with it - sounds like you're volunteering to review it, so I'll get it rebased and send out a patch in a few days.
can you share the branch so I can pick your patch? I am rebasing some stuff and I'd take whatever you have if available
but yeah 100% on the review part, just ping me and I'll have a look
I'm not near that machine but I'll share it as soon as I can

Hey! Sorry for the delay. I finally rebased this but haven't gone over yet to see that it's fit for the mailing list. It's here: https://github.com/tamird/linux/tree/xarray-bindings-more let me know if this does what you want! cc @Alice Ryhl (IIRC rust binder needs this too)

Let me hijack this thread.
I'm interested in doing preallocations for XArray (reserve an entry, store later in atomic context without allocating)
Is there a solution for that yet?
Is your xarray operation the first operation in your critical region?
doesn't really matter as long as it does not fail
If you can make it the first operation, then you can just unlock the spinlock, do the allocation, and take the spinlock again to retry when allocations are needed.
